mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in industrial production signals improving near-term output and operating leverage, raising expected earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot available at decision time was 96.8202 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by non-positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above its recent trend signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing investors to lower earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot available at decision time was 8.1 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment deteriorations are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate over the prior 3 months raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, reducing valuation support and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change is greater than 0.25 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot available at decision time was 0.13 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS increases greater than 0.25 percentage points are followed by positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if the negative return spread versus non-tightening months is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth above its 6-month trend signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum, increasing expected corporate cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses above its trailing 6-month average growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot available at decision time was 406748.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered retail-sales accelerations are not followed by higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered months, or if the hit rate is below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below its 12-month average signals weakening residential investment and future durable-goods demand, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot available at decision time was 746.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdowns greater than 10% are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if returns after triggers are not below unconditional returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales acceleration combined with non-accelerating CPI identifies real demand growth rather than inflation-only sales gains, supporting margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth is below its trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot available at decision time was 230.102 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-positive and CPI-nonaccelerating state does not outperform RSAFS-positive-only states over 20 trading days, or if interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth during a high-unemployment, low-policy-rate regime signals recovery without immediate monetary tightening pressure, improving expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive while UNRATE is above 7% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot available at decision time was 133300.0 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with zero-bound monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS signals under UNRATE above 7% and FEDFUNDS below 1% fail to deliver positive mean 60-day S&P 500 returns or underperform comparable PAYEMS signals outside the regime.

mechanism_narrative: CPI acceleration while the policy rate is not rising signals a negative real-rate shock and future tightening risk, pressuring valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its trailing 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS 3-month change is less than 0.10 percentage points
regime_hint: inflation scare with delayed policy response
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration with stable FEDFUNDS is followed by non-negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal loses a negative hit rate above 52% in rolling walk-forward tests.
